channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mudavadi Explains Nairobi–National Government Partnership to Senate Committee
item.description: Prime Cabinet Secretary Musalia Mudavadi on Monday appeared before the Senate Committee on Devolution and Intergovernmental Relations to shed light on the recently signed Intergovernmental Cooperation...

item.title: Bandits Attack Dangoro Village in Zamfara, Kill Three and Burn Homes
item.description: On Sunday night, Dangoro village for Nahuce Ward, Bungudu Local Government Area, Zamfara State, suffer heavy attack from armed bandits. Residents report say three people die, including one member of t...

item.title: How Age Gaps Shape Sibling Relationships and Rivalry
item.description: The SBS Insight article explores the complexities of sibling relationships and how differences in age, upbringing and life stages can lead to rivalry or estrangement. Lea, the youngest of three siblin...

item.title: Bafana Bafana coach confirms most of World Cup squad selection
item.description: Bafana Bafana coach Hugo Broos has indicated that his final squad for the upcoming FIFA World Cup is largely set, with about 75% of the players already decided in his mind. Speaking at a press confere...

item.title: Gerald Anderson immerses himself deeply to authentically portray Muslim culture in 'Blood vs Duty'
item.description: Actor Gerald Anderson has shown remarkable dedication in his portrayal of a Muslim character in the new ABS-CBN series “Blood vs Duty.” The show, which explores themes of conflict, loyalty, and family...

item.title: BIS panel recommends keeping fire safety provisions in National Building Code
item.description: NEW DELHI: Following a series of deadly building fires in Delhi, Odisha, and Goa, the technical committee of the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) has recommended that fire safety provisions remain par...

item.title: Water security challenges shaping South Africa’s manufacturing sector
item.description: Water security has become a critical factor influencing South Africa’s economic and industrial trajectory. Despite significant improvements in water and sanitation access supported by national policie...

item.title: Peso weakens to record 60.69 per dollar amid Middle East conflict and oil disruptions
item.description: The Philippine peso closed at a record-low exchange rate of ₱60.69 to the US dollar on Monday, March 30, 2026, reflecting mounting market concerns over the escalating war in the Middle East. The confl...

item.title: Everyday movements can boost health and fight lifestyle diseases
item.description: This article discusses new research from South Africa showing that small, everyday movements can have meaningful health benefits, even without a formal exercise routine. The country faces a growing cr...

item.title: Sai Sudharsan and Shubman Gill Impress with Dedication Ahead of IPL 2026 Clash
item.description: Gujarat Titans opener B. Sai Sudharsan was one of the standout performers in IPL 2025, earning the Orange Cap with an impressive tally of 759 runs. Despite his previous success, Sudharsan is committed...

item.title: South African activists urge government to oppose US-Israel military actions in Iran
item.description: The South African chapter of the Anti-Apartheid Movement has publicly called for decisive action against what it terms the 'illegal war' waged by the United States and Israel against Iran. The organis...

item.title: Senator Ibrahim-Khalid Mustapha resigns from PDP over internal party crisis
item.description: Senator Ibrahim-Khalid Mustapha, wey dey represent Kaduna North Senatorial District, don resign from di Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) because him say di party don dey face plenty wahala like leadersh...

item.title: Federal government rejects Dartmouth Cove infilling project due to environmental concerns
item.description: The federal government of Canada has rejected a controversial proposal to infill part of Halifax Harbour at Dartmouth Cove, a move that sought to support a private development initiative. Fisheries an...

item.title: Economists Warn Fuel Excise Cut May Increase Inflation Despite Short-Term Relief
item.description: The Albanese government has announced a three-month halving of the fuel excise and a zeroing of the heavy vehicle road user charge, aiming to provide short-term relief to households facing high fuel c...

item.title: U.S. Veteran Candidates Split Over Trump's Iran War Strategy
item.description: As the U.S.-Israel war with Iran enters its second month, military veteran candidates across both political parties are reshaping their congressional campaigns around the realities of a nation at war....

item.title: IMF Warns Iran Conflict Could Raise Global Prices and Slow Economic Growth
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has issued a warning that the ongoing war in the Middle East, triggered by US and Israeli strikes on Iran on February 28, 2026, is causing serious disruptions to ...

item.title: Health Minister engages stakeholders amid legal challenges to NHI Act
item.description: Health Minister Aaron Motsoaledi has recently met with the South African Medical Association (Sama) to explore common ground regarding the National Health Insurance (NHI) Act, which is facing multiple...

item.title: UP GE Club rebrands national geodetic summit to foster collaboration in engineering and geomatics
item.description: The University of the Philippines Geodetic Engineering Club (UP GE Club) is celebrating a milestone with the launch of the National Geodetic Engineering Summit (NatGES) 2026. The two-day event, happen...

item.title: Greenpeace warns of humanitarian and nuclear risks if Iran’s power grid is attacked
item.description: Greenpeace International has strongly condemned former President Donald Trump's threats to target Iran's electricity infrastructure, emphasizing that such actions could provoke a severe humanitarian c...

item.title: Canadian Officials Alert Public About Fraud Risks Ahead of FIFA World Cup
item.description: Canadian authorities are issuing warnings about potential fraud and scams as the FIFA World Cup nears, urging citizens to be vigilant and cautious. The upcoming international tournament, which draws l...
